RWR-1.finding: Verdict "Non-discriminating" preserved verbatim at the start of the rewritten text. The directional verdict (dome scaling works mechanically but does not discriminate from standard Sq + alternative-H(r) families) is unchanged.

RWR-2.finding: Load-bearing nouns preserved: "Chapman Sq ionospheric framework", "FSF = H(r)/r", "BOU 2017 −10.9 nT", "in-sample evaluation", "Kp precondition", "post-hoc test", "Coordinate Collapse", "9-station discrimination test". The claim structure (eight-part chain summarised in CONTENT_PRESERVATION_AUDIT) reads the same in both texts.

RWR-3.finding: 2 minor drops noted under holes_found (causal "because" + one adversative "but"). Three adversative "but" instances and the existing "because FSF is now confirmed" sentence-initial connective survived unchanged. The argument chain reads in every case; no break.

RWR-4.reason: no-Category-D-restructure-applied (rewriter used A+C only — sentence splitting at semicolon/em-dash boundaries, no paragraph reordering)

RWR-5.finding: No new factual claims, numbers, or citations introduced. Substitutions are syntactic ("and" → period+new-sentence, "whose" → "Their", "yielding" → "That yields"). Spot-checks on bigram diff confirmed all additions are sentence-boundary/pronoun-bridge artifacts.

RWR-6.finding: All 40 numbers in CONTENT_PRESERVATION_AUDIT preserved (verified by grep). All 17 citations preserved verbatim (same DOI, same author-year, same context anchors). Kernel-of-truth setup preserved: "Credit where it is due on the scaling itself: the field-strength factor genuinely does equal H(r)/r normalized to Boulder" — exact text. Caveats preserved: exhaustivity-check open, nonlinear-coverage sensitivity check pre-registered, K&C drop-off upper bound flagged as pre-registration.

RWR-7.finding: Each citation anchors the same claim in both texts. Chapman (1919) → framework formulation; Yamazaki & Maute (2017) → modern conductivity treatment; Kim & Chang (2018) → 39-eclipse INTERMAGNET confirmation; DOI link → same K&C paper; john09289 link → dome author definition of timestamping; #section-1-8 link → Coordinate Collapse. No citation re-anchored to a different claim.

RWR-8.finding: Every number stays attached to the same physical quantity. Sample: −10.9 nT still attached to BOU 2017 anchor; 8537/8619 still attached to V12 coordinate equation; 0.642–2.075 still attached to dome FSF values; 0.6%/2.78%/0.9999 still attached to station match accuracy; nine European station predictions list (EBR…COI) verbatim; Kp ≈ 3 / Kp < 2 still attached to 2017 baseline / 2026 validation; ≤30% / >50° / ≳58° still attached to CGM drop-off / LER respectively.

RWR-9.finding: Predicted sentence_complexity -5 → -1 is verified: programmatic count gives 20 long sentences (>35w) in original, 4 in rewritten. Floor(20/4)=5 → floor(4/4)=1. Word count 1455 → 1458 (claimed +3, actual +3). Length axis remains at 0 (still ~4.86× target_max=300) as the rewriter acknowledges — the surface is long because it has content density, not structural verbosity. Heuristic method is internally consistent with the actual rewrite product.

holes_found.description: Causal connective "because" dropped on the H(r)/r alternative-parametrizations explanation. The rewritten text splits a single causal sentence into two adjacent assertions; the explanatory chain still reads by adjacency, but the explicit "because" linkage is gone.
holes_found.evidence: Original: "...should produce closely-similar FSF curves over the dome's 0–14,000 km station-radius range, because H(r)/r over a finite range is constrained primarily by the calibration anchors rather than the specific interpolating family." Rewritten: "...should produce closely-similar FSF curves over the dome's 0–14,000 km station-radius range. H(r)/r over a finite range is constrained primarily by the calibration anchors rather than the specific interpolating family."
holes_found.recommendation: Optional fix: "Because H(r)/r over a finite range is constrained primarily by the calibration anchors rather than the specific interpolating family, alternative…" — or accept the adjacency-based reading. Approve does not block on this.

holes_found.description: Adversative "but" dropped on the rigorous-Sq-vs-bare-multiplier methodological caveat. Without "but," the relationship between the rigorous Sq description and the methodological choice to use the simpler linear-coverage form is implicit by adjacency. The reader has to infer that we are acknowledging the more rigorous treatment exists while choosing the simpler symmetric form deliberately.
holes_found.evidence: Original: "...A rigorous Sq treatment enters coverage through the ionospheric-conductivity integral with altitude and local-time structure rather than as a bare multiplier — but we adopt it symmetrically so the residual comparison does not bias either direction…" Rewritten: "A rigorous Sq treatment enters coverage through the ionospheric-conductivity integral with altitude and local-time structure, rather than as a bare multiplier. We adopt the linear form symmetrically so the residual comparison does not bias either direction."
holes_found.recommendation: Optional fix: insert "However," or "Even so," before "We adopt the linear form symmetrically...". Approve does not block on this.

curmudgeon_open_concerns: Length axis is untouched (still scored 0 / ~4.86× target_max). The rewriter acknowledges this is outside A-E remediation scope. If readability of WIN-010.detail_verdict_text is still a downstream concern after this rewrite lands, future passes may need to consider whether the section can be excerpted/chunked or whether the math-dense / content-dense flag should be applied. Pre-existing oddity (not introduced by the rewrite): the " — a crude but falsifiable nine-number competitor" em-dash fragment appears mid-paragraph as a dangling appositive in BOTH original and rewritten. The rewriter did not touch it; it is grammatically orphaned in both texts but the meaning is recoverable.
summary_for_decider: Approved. Category A (19 sentence splits) and Category C (one em-dash citation-cluster split) applied cleanly. All 40 numbers and 17 citations preserved verbatim. Verdict "Non-discriminating" + 8-part argument chain preserved. RWR-9 calibration verified: 20→4 long sentences exactly as claimed. Two minor RWR-3 connective drops noted ("because" + one adversative "but") — within the 0–2 minor budget. Decider can integrate.
